book_claims.claim: Black beans are used in the Longevity Diet as a primary plant protein source (e.g., pasta with broccoli and black beans), recommended at 150 g wet weight (boiled, drained) per serving.
book_claims.mechanism: Provides complete amino acid spectrum when paired with grains; plant protein source associated with longevity in centenarian populations.

book_claims.claim: Black beans are a notable calcium source (102 mg per cup cooked, ~10% DV) and magnesium source (60 mg per 1/2 cup cooked, ~15% DV).
book_claims.mechanism: Calcium supports bone mineral density; magnesium is required for ATP synthesis, DNA repair, and over 300 enzymatic reactions.

supplementary_research.source: Bazzano et al., Archives of Internal Medicine (2001) — PMID 11176744
supplementary_research.finding: In a prospective study of 9,632 US adults, legume consumption 4+ times per week versus less than once per week was associated with a 22% lower risk of coronary heart disease and 11% lower risk of cardiovascular disease, establishing legumes as a core longevity food.

supplementary_research.source: Messina, American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (2014) — PMID 24898230
supplementary_research.finding: Legumes including black beans contain resistant starch, indigestible fiber, and polyphenols (anthocyanins in black beans) that collectively reduce postprandial glucose, feed beneficial microbiota, and reduce systemic inflammation — key pathways in longevity.

supplementary_research.source: Aparicio-Fernandez et al., Plant Foods for Human Nutrition (2005) — PMID 16167185
supplementary_research.finding: Black bean seed coat is exceptionally rich in anthocyanins (particularly delphinidin-3-glucoside) and other polyphenols with in vitro antioxidant capacity comparable to or exceeding blueberries, supporting cardiovascular and anti-aging protection.

aroma: slightly smoky when cooked with aromatics
texture: slightly firm skin when cooked al dente

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: PDCAAS ~0.75; improved when combined with grains; lower in sulfur amino acids, so pair with methionine-rich foods.
High in soluble and insoluble fiber; feeds Bifidobacterium and Faecalibacterium prausnitzii, key longevity-associated microbes.
Primarily in seed coat; partially degraded by cooking; remaining fraction is bioavailable and acts as antioxidant.
High natural folate content; important for DNA synthesis, methylation, and homocysteine regulation.

synergies.description: Black beans and broccoli together cover essential amino acids, calcium, and vitamin C in a single dish, forming a nutritionally complete longevity meal component.
Classic complementary protein pairing; together they provide all essential amino acids, including methionine (low in beans) and lysine (low in rice).
Olive oil increases carotenoid and fat-soluble polyphenol absorption from the meal.
